'''George King''' (born 24 February 1995) is an [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|lf}} and {{rlp|pr}} for the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the [[Super League]].

He previously played for [[Wakefield Trinity]] and the [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[Super League]], and on loan from [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] at the [[North Wales Crusaders]] in [[2015 Championship 1|Championship 1]] and the [[Rochdale Hornets]] in the [[2018 Betfred Championship|Betfred Championship]].<ref name="Profile at loverugbyleague.com"/><ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Background==
King was born in [[Huddersfield]], [[West Yorkshire]], England.

==Playing career==
George is the older brother of [[Toby King (rugby league)|Toby King]]: they both played their amateur rugby league at Meltham All Blacks, and George additionally played for Siddal. They were spotted by the Wires’ Yorkshire scout Tommy Gleeson after both appeared for Huddersfield.{{citation needed|date=March 2020}}

In 2016 he was called up to the [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] squad for the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup qualification – Europe#Group B|2017 Rugby League World Cup European Pool B qualifiers]].<ref name="Warrington Wolves trio named in Ireland squad for World Cup qualifiers">{{cite web|url=http://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/14763089.Warrington_Wolves_trio_named_in_Ireland_squad_for_World_Cup_qualifiers|title=Warrington Wolves trio named in Ireland squad for World Cup qualifiers|publisher=Warrington Guardian|date=24 September 2016|access-date=26 September 2016}}</ref>

===Warrington Wolves===
King was promoted to the [[Warrington Wolves]] first team squad in 2014.<ref>{{cite web|last1=Gordon|first1=James|title=Brothers promoted to Wolves first-team squad|url=http://www.loverugbyleague.com/news_14580-brothers-promoted-to-wolves-first-team-squad.html|publisher=Love Rugby League|access-date=23 July 2014|date=24 March 2014}}</ref> His Super League début was against the [[Bradford Bulls]] in June 2014.

He played in the [[2016 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by [[Hull F.C.]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Hull FC’s Jamie Shaul’s late try takes Challenge Cup away from Warrington">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2016/aug/27/hull-warrington-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Hull FC's Jamie Shaul's late try takes Challenge Cup away from Warrington|publisher=Guardian|date=27 August 2016|access-date=7 November 2019}}</ref><ref name="Challenge Cup final: Hull FC 12-10 Warrington Wolves">{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/37169016|title=Challenge Cup final: Hull FC 12-10 Warrington Wolves|publisher=BBC|date=27 August 2016|access-date=7 November 2019}}</ref>

He played in the [[2016 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by the [[Wigan Warriors]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="Warrington 6-12 Wigan: Super League Grand Final – as it happened!">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2016/oct/08/super-league-grand-final-warrington-wigan-live|title=Warrington 6-12 Wigan: Super League Grand Final – as it happened!|publisher=Guardian|date=8 October 2016|access-date=7 November 2019}}</ref>

He played in the [[2018 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by the [[Catalans Dragons]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Catalans Dragons beat Warrington in Challenge Cup final to make history">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2018/aug/25/catalans-dragons-warrington-wolves-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Catalans Dragons beat Warrington in Challenge Cup final to make history|publisher=Guardian|date=25 August 2018|access-date=5 November 2019}}</ref>

He played in the [[2018 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by the [[Wigan Warriors]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="Super League Grand Final 2018: Wigan 12-4 Warrington – as it happened">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2018/oct/13/super-league-grand-final-2018-wigan-warriors-v-warrington-wolves-live?page=with:block-5bc1ff43e4b05798be94cc63#liveblog-navigation|title=Super League Grand Final 2018: Wigan 12-4 Warrington – as it happened|publisher=Guardian|date=13 October 2018|access-date=5 November 2019}}</ref>

===Hull Kingston Rovers===
In September 2020 the club web site announced the signing of King from [[Wakefield Trinity]]<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.hullkr.co.uk/rovers-secure-the-signature-of-george-king-until-2022/|title=ROVERS SECURE THE SIGNATURE OF GEORGE KING UNTIL 2022|website=www.hullkr.co.uk|date=27 September 2020 |access-date=15 Dec 2020}}</ref>
King played a total of 20 games for Hull KR in the [[2021 Super League season]] including the club's 28-10 semi-final loss against the [[Catalans Dragons]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2021/sep/30/catalans-dragons-hull-kr-super-league-play-off-semi-final-match-report-rugby-league/|title=Catalans Dragons blast past Hull KR and into their first Super League Grand Final|newspaper=The Guardian|date=30 September 2021 |last1=Bower |first1=Aaron }}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12204/12415081/barrie-mcdermotts-2021-betfred-super-league-season-review/|title=Barrie McDermott's 2021 Betfred Super League season review|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/58663060.amp/|title=Super League: Warrington Wolves 0-19 Hull KR - Robins set up a semi-final at Catalans|website=www.bbc.com|date=23 September 2021 }}</ref>
On 12 August 2023, King played for Hull Kingston Rovers in their 17-16 golden point extra-time loss to [[Leigh Leopards|Leigh]] in the Challenge Cup final.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66463438|title=Challenge Cup final: Hull KR 16-17 Leigh Leopards - Leigh claim first Wembley final win in 52 years|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>
King played 27 games for Hull Kingston Rovers in the 2023 Super League season as the club finished fourth on the table and qualified for the playoffs. He played in the clubs semi-final loss against Wigan.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/live/rugby-league/67008107|title=Relive Wigan's thumping win v Hull KR to head to Grand Final|work=BBC Sport |date=6 October 2023 }}</ref>

===Huddersfield Giants===
On 17 October 2024 it was reported that he had signed for [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] in the Super League on a four-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=17 Oct 2024|author=Callum Walker|title=Hull KR prop George King makes move to Super League rivals|url=https://www.totalrl.com/hull-kr-prop-george-king-makes-move-to-super-league-rivals/}}</ref>
King played 18 matches for Huddersfield in the 2025 Super League season as the club finished 10th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/huddersfield-giants-coach-sounds-nrl-warning-as-honest-admission-made-over-2025-shortcomings/|title=Huddersfield Giants coach sounds NRL warning as honest admission made over 2025 shortcomings|website=www.loverugbyleague.com}}</ref>
